metadata.title: Data Flywheel (R4)
metadata.description: Adoption produces proprietary interaction data — agent traces, preferences, error corrections — that competitors cannot see. The lock-in mechanism most likely to tip the system to a single winner.
metadata.author: Tracking AI Course

nodes.label: Model Capability
nodes.description: Aggregate quality of the frontier model.

nodes.label: User Adoption
nodes.description: Number of active users, agents, and enterprise integrations running on the model.

nodes.label: Proprietary Interaction Data
nodes.description: Private signal from real-world usage: traces, preferences, error corrections — not available to competitors.

nodes.label: Training Signal Quality
nodes.description: Quality of the data used to fine-tune and post-train the model.

edges.description: Better models attract more users and agents.
Real usage produces unique training signal that did not exist before.
Proprietary data raises the quality of the training signal.
Better training signal raises model capability.

loops.label: R4: Data Flywheel
loops.description: Public benchmarks plateau; private data flywheels do not. Even if a competitor matches the architecture, they cannot match the data trail.
loops.behavior_pattern: Compounding capability advantage invisible to competitors.
